rationale_for_baseline_tier: An uncomplicated hordeolum with a focal lid-margin lesion, normal vision and painless eye movements is self-manageable with warm compresses (T0). Preseptal/orbital cellulitis carries a hard T4 safety-net.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Redness/swelling spreading across the whole eyelid or cheek, double vision, pain on moving the eye, a bulging eye, reduced vision, or fever
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Same day — urgent eye care / ED
escalation_edges.rationale: Suggests preseptal or orbital cellulitis — sight/life-threatening.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the redness spreads across your whole eyelid or cheek, you get double vision, pain moving the eye, a bulging eye, blurred vision or a fever — get urgent eye care or go to ED that day.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Give clear orbital red flags for self-monitoring.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Use warm compresses for about 10 minutes four times a day and keep your hands clean — don't squeeze it. It should point and drain within a couple of days.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Self-care; urgent eye care/ED for periorbital spread or orbital signs
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Urgent eye care/ED for spreading redness, double vision or vision change at any time.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Uncomplicated right upper-lid hordeolum; preseptal/orbital cellulitis screened negative; warm compresses; do-not-squeeze advice.

critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An evolving orbital cellulitis is dismissed as a stye and progresses to vision loss.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: No do-not-squeeze warning, risking dangerous spread.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: ED/ambulance for a simple stye with a negative orbital screen — unnecessary.
